summary: Pre-Raphaelite-adjacent painter. Known for depictions of The Lady of Shalott (red-haired woman in white dress in boat), Ophelia (sitting on tree branch over river), Gather Ye Rosebuds While Ye May, and La Belle Dame sans Merci.

works.name: General
works.description: Pre-Raphaelite-adjacent painter of literary and mythological subjects. Key works: The Lady of Shalott (red-haired woman floating in boat), Ophelia (on tree branch over pond with lilypads), Gather Ye Rosebuds While Ye May, La Belle Dame sans Merci.

clues.clue: The Lady of Shalott: red-haired woman in white dress sitting on quilt, floating in a boat
examples: depicted a red-haired woman in a white dress sitting on top of a quilt while floating in a boat in one of his depictions of the Lady of Shalott

clues.clue: Ophelia: sitting on tree branch next to pond (distinct from Millais's version where Siddall nearly died of hypothermia)
examples: A Waterhouse painting of this Shakespeare character shows her sitting on a tree next to a pond

clues.clue: La Belle Dame sans Merci: maroon-clad character wrapping a scarf around a man she's about to kiss
examples: Waterhouse depicted this maroon-clad character wrapping a tan scarf around a man she is about to kiss

comprehensive_summary: John William Waterhouse was a Pre-Raphaelite-adjacent painter primarily known for literary and mythological subjects. His Lady of Shalott depicts a red-haired woman in a white dress sitting on a quilt while floating in a boat. His Ophelia shows the Shakespeare character sitting on a tree branch over a lilypad-covered river (distinct from Millais's version). He painted Gather Ye Rosebuds While Ye May (women in pink and blue), and La Belle Dame sans Merci (maroon-clad woman wrapping a scarf around a man).

cards.front: Painting: red-haired woman in white dress floating in a boat, based on a Tennyson poem
cards.back: The Lady of Shalott (John William Waterhouse)

cards.front: Painting: Shakespeare character sitting on a tree branch over a lilypad-covered river (not the Millais version)
